items.title: Data acquisition
items.description: Download the Kaggle House Prices dataset (train.csv, test.csv, data_description.txt) and set it up in the repo. Do not commit the raw CSVs to git — add them to .gitignore and document how to obtain them. Write a download script instead.
acceptanceCriteria: src/data/download.py exists and downloads train.csv and test.csv to data/raw/ when run
data/raw/ is in .gitignore
data/README.md documents all 81 columns with type and description
Running download.py end-to-end produces data/raw/train.csv (1460 rows) and data/raw/test.csv (1459 rows)

items.title: EDA notebook
items.description: Exploratory data analysis on the house prices dataset. Visualise distributions, correlations, and missing values. Summarise the top findings as markdown cells so they serve as documentation.
acceptanceCriteria: notebooks/01_eda.ipynb exists and runs end-to-end without errors
Notebook includes: SalePrice distribution (raw + log), missing value heatmap, correlation matrix of numeric features, top-15 features most correlated with SalePrice
Markdown cells summarise the 3–5 most important findings for downstream feature engineering
Notebook is committed with output cells cleared (clean diff)

items.title: Preprocessing pipeline
items.description: Build a reusable preprocessing pipeline that handles missing values and encodes categorical features. This will be imported by all model scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: src/features.py exists with a build_pipeline() function that returns a sklearn Pipeline
Pipeline handles all missing values (numeric: median imputation, categorical: most-frequent imputation)
Pipeline one-hot encodes all categorical columns
Pipeline applies log1p transform to SalePrice target
tests/test_features.py exists with at least 3 unit tests that pass with pytest
Running src/features.py as a script prints train/val shapes confirming the pipeline works

items.title: Baseline linear regression model
items.description: Train a linear regression baseline using the preprocessing pipeline from task-003. Log results and establish the benchmark to beat.
acceptanceCriteria: src/model_baseline.py exists and runs end-to-end without errors
Uses the pipeline from src/features.py
Trains on 80% of train.csv, evaluates on 20% validation split
Logs results to results/baseline.json with fields: experiment, model, val_rmse, val_mae, train_rmse, hyperparams, timestamp, branch
EXPERIMENTS.md updated with a new row for this experiment
Val RMSE is logged (no specific target — just establish the number)

items.title: Gradient boosting model (XGBoost or LightGBM)
items.description: Train a gradient boosting model using the same preprocessing pipeline. Compare against the baseline and document findings.
acceptanceCriteria: src/model_gbm.py exists and runs end-to-end without errors
Uses XGBoost or LightGBM (whichever is easier to install via uv)
Uses the pipeline from src/features.py
Same 80/20 split as baseline for fair comparison
Logs results to results/gbm.json with same schema as baseline
EXPERIMENTS.md updated with a new row
Val RMSE is at least 5% lower than baseline (relative improvement)

items.title: Experiment comparison script
items.description: Write a script that reads all results/*.json files and prints a ranked comparison table. This becomes the single source of truth for 'what's the best model so far'.
acceptanceCriteria: src/compare.py exists and runs end-to-end without errors
Reads all JSON files from results/ directory
Prints a table sorted by val_rmse ascending (best first) with columns: rank, experiment, model, val_rmse, val_mae, date
Updates the 'Best Known RMSE' line in EXPERIMENTS.md to reflect the current best
Works even if results/ is empty (prints 'No experiments yet')
